mechanism: Proposed mechanism: phytoestrogenic activity via isoflavone constituents (estrogen-like structural similarity).
activeCompounds: Isoflavones (do not specify individual isoflavones without additional Tier‑1 chemistry references).
safetyNotes: NCCIH reports red clover extracts used in studies up to 2 years with apparent safety and generally well tolerated; may be unsafe in pregnancy/breastfeeding.
description: Legume; contains isoflavones structurally similar to estrogen; supplements promoted for women’s health.
preparation: Extracts/capsules marketed as dietary supplements for menopause-related symptoms and other uses.
region: POWO lists native range including Macaronesia, NW. Africa, Europe to Mongolia and Himalaya.
summary: Studied mainly for menopausal symptoms and lipids; NCCIH finds evidence inconsistent and not conclusive for benefits; may have cholesterol effects but more research needed.
